github.copilot.chat.codeGeneration.instr.text: When a test is failing, read the type contract (core-types/src/**) BEFORE editing the test. If the test assertion format disagrees with the declared type, fix one side — do not regex-sweep the other.
Never create fix-*.cjs, fix-*.js, patch-*.sh, or similar throwaway helper scripts inside packages/*/ directories. One-shot transforms are run-and-delete in the same turn, not committed as files.
Do not generate variants of the same fix (fix-foo.ts, fix-foo-v2.ts, fix-foo-final.ts). If a first attempt fails, stop regex-iterating and re-read the type contract.

github.copilot.chat.testGeneration.instr.text: Tests assert a contract. Before changing test assertion shape (tuple vs object, promise vs value, etc.) verify the corresponding declared type in core-types/src/. If they disagree, the declared type is the source of truth — edit the implementation to match, not the test to match the implementation.

github.copilot.chat.reviewSelection.inst.text: Flag any PR containing fix-*.cjs, fix-*.js, or patch-*.sh files added to packages/ directories — these are regex-sweep anti-patterns. Flag any PR that changes N test files without a corresponding type or implementation change.
